creator: dch@freebsd.org
text:
thanks Joel.

I'm not sure that freebsd-update is supported in this way. I'll let
others chime in on that.

> doas freebsd-update -r 14.2-RELEASE -b /tmp/stuff upgrade
src component not installed, skipped
freebsd-update: Cannot upgrade from a version that is not a release
(including alpha, beta and release candidates)
using freebsd-update. Instead, FreeBSD can be directly
upgraded by source or upgraded to a RELEASE/RELENG version
prior to running freebsd-update.
Currently running: 15.0-CURRENT

For your use case instead, use the jail-specific functionality built into fbsd-update:

e.g. for a jail called stuff:

# freebsd-update -r 14.2-RELEASE -j stuff upgrade

- used a 14.1-RELEASE jail
- from a 15.0-CURRENT host
- to update to 14.2-RELEASE
- and then again to patch to p0

# freebsd-update -r 14.2-RELEASE -j stuff upgrade
# freebsd-update -r 14.2-RELEASE -j stuff install
# freebsd-update -j stuff install
# freebsd-update -j stuff fetch install
...
No updates needed to update system to 14.2-RELEASE-p0.
No updates are available to install.

If the directory you want to upgrade is not an active jail, either jail
it ;-) or run the upgrade within the jail dir as chroot.
